abstract-factory.intent: Provide an interface for creating families of related objects without specifying their concrete classes

adapter.intent: Convert the interface of a class into another interface clients expect, enabling incompatible interfaces to work together

add-parameter.intent: Add a parameter to a method so it can receive information it currently cannot access

bridge.intent: Decouple an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently

builder.intent: Separate the construction of a complex object from its representation so the same process can create different representations

chain-of-responsibility.intent: Pass a request along a chain of handlers; each handler decides to process it or forward it

clean-architecture.intent: Organize code in concentric layers where dependencies point inward, keeping business rules independent of frameworks

change-bidirectional-association-to-unid.intent: Remove one direction of a two-way link when only one end needs to navigate to the other

change-reference-to-value.intent: Convert a reference object that has no shared-identity requirement into an immutable value object

change-unidirectional-association-to-bid.intent: Add a reverse pointer to a one-way association when both ends need to navigate to each other

change-value-to-reference.intent: Convert a value object to a reference object so that multiple owners share one instance with consistent identity

collapse-hierarchy.intent: Merge a superclass and subclass that are no longer different enough to justify a two-level hierarchy

command.intent: Encapsulate a request as an object, enabling parameterization, queuing, logging, and undo/redo

cqrs.intent: Separate read and write models so each can be optimised, scaled, and evolved independently

composite.intent: Compose objects into tree structures to represent part-whole hierarchies and let clients treat individual objects and compositions uniformly

consolidate-conditional-expression.intent: Merge multiple separate conditionals with the same result into one combined condition, then extract it as a method

consolidate-duplicate-conditional-fragme.intent: Move code that appears in every branch of a conditional outside the conditional so it executes unconditionally

decompose-conditional.intent: Extract the condition and each branch of a complex conditional into named methods that reveal their intent

dependency-injection.intent: Supply a component's dependencies from the outside rather than letting it construct or locate them itself

decorator.intent: Attach additional responsibilities to an object dynamically by wrapping it, as a flexible alternative to subclassing

duplicate-observed-data.intent: Copy GUI-layer data into a domain object and keep them synchronized, separating domain logic from presentation

encapsulate-collection.intent: Return an unmodifiable view of a collection field and provide explicit add/remove methods so the owning class controls all mutations

encapsulate-field.intent: Make a public field private and provide getter/setter methods to control external access

event-sourcing.intent: Persist state as an append-only sequence of domain events rather than mutable current-state snapshots

extract-class.intent: Split a class doing two jobs into two focused classes, each with a single clear responsibility

extract-interface.intent: Extract a subset of a class's interface into a separate interface type so clients depend only on what they use

extract-method.intent: Pull a code fragment into its own named method/function to improve readability and enable reuse

extract-subclass.intent: Create a subclass for a cluster of features that is only used by some instances of the class

extract-superclass.intent: Create a superclass and move common features of two or more classes into it

extract-variable.intent: Assign a complex expression to a well-named temporary variable to make the expression's purpose visible

facade.intent: Provide a simplified interface to a complex subsystem, reducing client coupling to its internals

factory-method.intent: Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses decide which class to instantiate

flyweight.intent: Share fine-grained objects to support large numbers of them efficiently by separating intrinsic from extrinsic state

form-template-method.intent: Unify similar algorithms in subclasses by extracting the common skeleton into the superclass and leaving the differing steps as abstract methods

hide-delegate.intent: Add a delegation method on a server object so clients don't need to know about the server's internal delegates

hide-method.intent: Reduce a method's visibility to the minimum access level required by its actual callers

hexagonal.intent: Isolate core business logic from external concerns by expressing dependencies as ports and plugging in adapters

inline-class.intent: Absorb a class that no longer carries enough responsibility into its most frequent collaborator

inline-method.intent: Replace a method call with the method body when the method's name adds no clarity over its content

inline-variable.intent: Replace a temporary variable that is assigned once with the expression it holds when the variable name adds no clarity

interpreter.intent: Define a grammar for a language and provide an interpreter that uses the grammar to process sentences

introduce-assertion.intent: Make an implicit assumption about program state explicit by adding an assertion that documents and enforces the precondition

introduce-foreign-method.intent: Add a method to a client class that acts as if it belonged to an unmodifiable server class

introduce-local-extension.intent: Create a subclass or wrapper around an unmodifiable server class to house methods the server should own

introduce-null-object.intent: Replace null checks with a no-op object that implements the expected interface with safe default behaviour

introduce-parameter-object.intent: Replace a recurring group of parameters with a single object that encapsulates them

iterator.intent: Provide a way to sequentially access elements of a collection without exposing its underlying representation

mediator.intent: Define an object that encapsulates how a set of objects interact, reducing direct dependencies between them

memento.intent: Capture and externalise an object's internal state so it can be restored later without violating encapsulation

move-field.intent: Relocate a field to the class that uses it most, so data lives beside the behavior that governs it

move-method.intent: Move a method to the class that uses it most, eliminating feature envy and placing behavior with its data

observer.intent: Define a one-to-many dependency so that when one object changes state, all dependents are notified automatically

parameterize-method.intent: Merge several methods that do similar things by extracting the differing literal values into parameters

preserve-whole-object.intent: Pass the whole object to a method instead of extracting several values from it before the call

prototype.intent: Specify the kinds of objects to create using a prototypical instance, and create new objects by copying that prototype

proxy.intent: Provide a substitute object that controls access to another object, adding behaviour before or after the real object is reached

pull-up-constructor-body.intent: Move common constructor initialization code from subclass constructors into a superclass constructor

pull-up-field.intent: Move a field that appears identically in two or more subclasses up to the common superclass

pull-up-method.intent: Move methods with identical or equivalent bodies from subclasses up to the superclass to eliminate duplication

push-down-field.intent: Move a field from the superclass to only those subclasses that actually use it

push-down-method.intent: Move a method from the superclass to only those subclasses that actually use it

remove-assignments-to-parameters.intent: Introduce a local variable for post-entry mutations instead of reassigning an incoming parameter

remove-control-flag.intent: Replace a boolean control-flag variable used to break out of loops or conditionals with break, return, or continue

remove-middle-man.intent: Delete a class that does nothing but forward calls, letting clients talk directly to the real object

remove-parameter.intent: Remove a parameter that is no longer used by the method body to shrink the caller contract

remove-setting-method.intent: Make a field immutable by removing its setter and initializing it only through the constructor

rename-method.intent: Change a method's name to better communicate its purpose and eliminate the need for comments

repository.intent: Mediate between domain and data-mapping layers using a collection-like interface for accessing domain objects

replace-array-with-object.intent: Replace an array used as a heterogeneous record with an object whose named fields make each slot's meaning explicit

replace-conditional-with-polymorphism.intent: Move each conditional branch into an overriding method on a subclass or type-case variant, eliminating the switch/if chain

replace-constructor-with-factory-method.intent: Replace a constructor call with a named factory method to communicate intent and decouple callers from concrete types

replace-data-value-with-object.intent: Promote a primitive or simple data item to an object so behavior related to it can live alongside the data

replace-delegation-with-inheritance.intent: Replace a field-plus-forwarding-methods pattern with direct inheritance when the class uses the full delegated interface

replace-error-code-with-exception.intent: Replace a special return value that signals failure with an exception so error paths are explicit and unignorable

replace-exception-with-test.intent: Replace an exception thrown for an avoidable condition with a guard check that prevents the exceptional state

replace-inheritance-with-delegation.intent: Replace a subclass relationship with a field holding the former superclass and explicit delegation calls

replace-magic-number-with-symbolic-const.intent: Replace a raw numeric literal that has a domain meaning with a named constant that makes the intent explicit

replace-method-with-method-object.intent: Turn a large method with many local variables into its own class where locals become fields and sub-steps become methods

replace-nested-conditional-with-guard-cl.intent: Use early returns for special/exceptional cases to eliminate nesting and highlight the main success path

replace-parameter-with-explicit-methods.intent: Split a method that switches on a parameter value into separate, explicitly named methods for each case

replace-parameter-with-method-call.intent: Remove a parameter by having the method invoke the computation itself instead of receiving the result

replace-record-with-data-class.intent: Replace a legacy record or struct with a proper class so behavior can be added to the data over time

replace-subclass-with-fields.intent: Collapse subclasses that differ only in constant-returning methods into fields on the parent class

replace-temp-with-query.intent: Replace a temporary variable that holds a computed value with a call to a query method computing that value

replace-type-code-with-class.intent: Replace an integer or string type code with a class so the type system validates legal values at compile time

replace-type-code-with-state-strategy.intent: Replace a mutable type code with a pluggable state or strategy object so behavior can change at runtime

replace-type-code-with-subclasses.intent: Replace a type code that drives conditional behavior with a subclass hierarchy, moving each branch into its own class

saga.intent: Coordinate a multi-step business transaction across services using a sequence of local transactions with compensating actions

self-encapsulate-field.intent: Access a field only through getter/setter methods even within its own class, enabling subclass override of access

separate-query-from-modifier.intent: Split a method that both returns a value and changes state into a pure query and a void modifier

singleton.intent: Ensure a class has only one instance and provide a global access point to it

split-temporary-variable.intent: Give each distinct usage of a temporary variable its own variable with a name matching its role

state.intent: Allow an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes, appearing to change its class

strategy.intent: Define a family of algorithms, encapsulate each one, and make them interchangeable at runtime

substitute-algorithm.intent: Replace the body of a method with a cleaner algorithm that produces the same results

template-method.intent: Define the skeleton of an algorithm in a base class, deferring some steps to subclasses without changing its structure

visitor.intent: Separate an algorithm from the object structure it operates on, letting new operations be added without modifying elements
